decision: Proceed with implementation after dependency gate check
reason: RD-101 depends on RD-002 and RD-002 has status DONE (5)
impact: Task is unblocked; implementation can continue in EPIC-INS scope

decision: Version installer contract and expose dedicated contract endpoint
reason: Downstream installer tasks require stable machine-readable backend interface before UI/scope work
impact: Backend now provides deterministic contract metadata via /api/pmo/installer/contract

decision: Implement fingerprint-based idempotency for install upsert
reason: Repeated install callbacks must be replay-safe and should not create noisy updates
impact: Identical payload replays return idempotent_replay=true and preserve existing installation record

decision: Use versioned mapping container in application_installation.status_code
reason: Need immediate mapping storage contract without schema migration blockers
impact: RD-102/RD-103 can evolve mapping data on top of a stable versioned structure

reason: Installer contract, idempotency, and mapping model baseline are implemented and validated
impact: EPIC-INS can proceed to mapping UI and scope/permissions checks

decision: Start with JSON-based mapping editor on settings page backed by dedicated mapping endpoints
reason: Fastest path to functional mapping workflow without blocking on final UX components
impact: Mapping state can now be read/updated end-to-end from UI to backend

decision: Expose scope/admin readiness check as dedicated backend endpoint and render in settings UI
reason: Permissions diagnostics must be explicit before running initial sync and deeper installer actions
impact: Portal readiness is visible and actionable from installer settings

decision: Make settings data loading resilient with Promise.allSettled and fallback payloads
reason: User saw stuck/loading settings screen in demo/degraded backend conditions
impact: Settings page now opens reliably and always shows RD-102 controls

decision: Remove backend health alert from index and replace with single configure CTA
reason: First user impression must be error-free and guide directly to configuration flow
impact: Home page no longer shows blocking error banner and routes user to RD-102 setup

decision: Replace JSON mapping editor with structured form rows
reason: User requested a working, human-friendly interface instead of raw JSON editing
impact: RD-102 flow is now operable through add/remove rows, numeric ID validation, and explicit save/reload actions

decision: Pivot RD-102 from mapping selection to guided entity creation wizard
reason: User requested deterministic setup flow that creates all base CRM entities instead of asking for manual mapping choices
impact: Settings now executes crm.automatedsolution.add then crm.type.add (Goals) with progress and links, reducing operator error

decision: Treat demo mode as simulation for create actions and surface actionable permission errors otherwise
reason: User encountered creation error while page was in demo mode and needed non-blocking UX
impact: In demo mode setup flow completes with simulated success; in real mode errors include scope hints and backend details

decision: Move RD-102 to TESTING after demo acceptance
reason: User confirmed all RD-102 setup steps pass in demo mode without errors
impact: Status advanced to TESTING and synchronized to Bitrix24; focus shifts to RD-103 and real-mode acceptance of RD-102

decision: Add explicit remediation guidance for missing scopes and admin access
reason: Permission checks should be actionable before creating fields in the Goals smart process
impact: Installer UI now explains what is missing and what to do next, reducing setup ambiguity

decision: Persist installer setup progress in application_installation.status_code.setup_state with dedicated GET/POST API
reason: Wizard state must survive reloads and allow resuming setup per installation
impact: Settings page now restores current step and created entities (DWS, Goals SP) from backend

decision: Finalize RD-107 with commit to master and mark Bitrix status sync for verification
reason: Need explicit closure trace including repository publication and operational caveats
impact: Task closure is auditable end-to-end and follow-up on board consistency is tracked
